text: In Agile methodology, teams work in short cycles called sprints, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint begins with planning, where tasks are selected from a list known as the backlog. These tasks are described as user stories, simple explanations of a feature from the user’s perspective. During the sprint, teams hold daily standups—brief meetings where each member shares what they did yesterday, what they plan to do today, and any obstacles. The key is iterative development: teams deliver small, usable parts of a product frequently, then improve them based on feedback. This approach helps teams adapt quickly to changes.
